seoTitle: How to Default a Power BI Date Slicer to Latest Date (2026)
metaDescription: Learn how to write a DAX column to auto-select the latest date in a Power BI slicer so dashboards always open on current data.
intro: Requiring users to manually scroll through thousands of rows to find today's date every time they open a dashboard is a poor experience. This guide shows how to write a simple DAX column that flags the latest date in your table, then use it to force a slicer to default to that value. You'll also learn how to keep the option open for users to still view historical data if needed.

steps.heading: Open the Data View
steps.body: Go to your Master Date or Transaction table in Data View and click New Column in the Table Tools ribbon.

steps.heading: Write the DAX flag formula
steps.body: Use an IF() statement that checks whether the current row's date equals MAX() of your date column, labeling matches as "Latest Date" and everything else as "Past Dates".

steps.heading: Add the slicer visual
steps.body: Return to your report canvas, drop a standard Slicer visual, and drag your new DAX column into its field well.

steps.heading: Set the default selection
steps.body: Check the "Latest Date" value in the slicer's list, then switch the layout style to Dropdown in the Format pane if preferred.

steps.heading: Lock the filter and test
steps.body: Open the Filters on this visual panel to lock the selection to your dynamic flag, then refresh your data to confirm the slicer advances automatically.

faq.question: Why doesn't a normal slicer update automatically to the latest date?
faq.answer: Standard static selections don't advance on their own when new date rows are imported, which is why a DAX-based flag column is needed.

faq.question: Can users still select older dates after I lock the default?
faq.answer: Yes, you can tune the visual options so users can still expand the slicer to view historical trends even though it defaults to the latest date.

faq.question: Does this method require a calculated column or a measure?
faq.answer: It typically uses a calculated column with an IF() and MAX() formula so each row can be flagged and used inside a slicer field well.

faq.question: How do I confirm the default updates after a refresh?
faq.answer: Add a new date to your source table, refresh the model, and check that the slicer's default selection moves to the newest date.
